238.reason: The regex for STRCON in Category.java is incorrect. It uses `[^\\\"\\\'\\\\]` which excludes single quotes and backslashes unnecessarily, and `\\\\n` which only matches literal `\n` instead of any escape sequence or non-special character. This causes valid string literals like "hello\a world\t" to fail the match, resulting in missing output for the string token.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The regex for STRCON in Category.java is incorrect. It uses `[^\\\"\\\'\\\\]` which excludes single quotes and backslashes unnecessarily, and `\\\\n` which only matches literal `\n` instead of any escape sequence or non-special character. This causes valid string literals like "hello\a world\t" to fail the match, resulting in missing output for the string token.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The regex for STRCON in Category.java is incorrect. It uses `[^\\\"\\\'\\\\]` which excludes single quotes and backslashes entirely, preventing it from matching valid escape sequences like `\a` or `\t` inside the string content part of the regex, or failing to match the structure correctly. Specifically, the regex `\\\"([^\\\"\\\'\\\\]|\\\\n)*\\\"` only allows `\\n` as an escape sequence, but the input contains `\a` and `\t`. The regex needs to be updated to support all valid escape sequences or a broader set of characters.
